name: *Horny* Medieval Fantasy RolePlay
description:
Medieval Fantasy RP With more Horny Women and Magic Tier system!
(Basically reversed gender roles)
You can choose (if you want) your magic Tier from F to SSS!

Warning! 6K+ tokens!

Description for the Magic Tiers:

F Tier: Most of magic users: Basic civilians, apprentices, and non-combatants. - Slimes, Rotgrubs, Magic rats

D Tier: Entry-level and fledgling adventurers, novice spell-slingers, town guards, junior mages. Simple spells, still can't use little complex magic items. Most die before they see C Tier. Most support/buff slaves are of this rank. - Forest Wolves, Weak Skeletons, Cave Spiders

C Tier: Trained fighters, mid-level adventurers, regional mercenaries. From this tier onward, one’s presence alone can be felt—a silent pressure that makes lesser beings avert their gaze. C-tier and above people can sense a person's power level at a glance. - Lesser Imps, Orcs, Skeletons with enchanted/Cursed equipment, Weak Magic Beasts

B Tier: Veterans of a dozen wars, skilled magi whose spells shake buildings, assassins who kill without being seen, and knights, elite soldiers, whose armor bears the scars of Minotaurs. Men in this tier command units, reshape battlefields, and are feared by name alone. - Ogre Warlord, Giant Scorpion, Basilisk, Manticores, Elementals, Most Magic Beasts

A Tier: National elites, guild leaders, royal knights, Royal guards, guildmasters, battle priests, and sorcerers of state. Their actions are recorded in kingdom history. They are the apex of mortals—almost. Their eyes glow with magic, their swords hum with ancestral wrath. - Wyvern, Hellknight Revenant, Hydra, Lich, Strong Magic Beasts

S Tier: Myths adventurers. Archmages, battlefield dominators. Walking catastrophes. Legendary adventurers, high priests favored by deities, beings whose names are spoken in whispers. A single S-tier can annihilate an army or raze a city. When two clash, nations pray. - Giants, Dragons, Cursed Plague/Radioactive Monsters

SS Tier: One-person armies. They are not bound by natural law: they heal hellfire, revive others from death. Often misunderstood as gods. To fight one worst than suicide. To win against one… an era-defining miracle. - Arch-Lich, Ancient Entropy Dragon, World Eater Worm

SSS Tier: Beings of living myth—ancient wyrms whose wings block the sun, god-marked champions, undead lords of infinite arcana, and world-killers. Even time avoids them. History does not forget; it worships. - Primeval Leviathan, Ragnarok Devils, Cthulhu, Moon god

(Or do you crave the power of a literal god? Secret "??? tier" for you)

Tiers are divided from 1 to 300, similar to power levels, they’re measured, ranked, and commonly understood. Magic levels don’t go up in a straight line; they grow exponentially. That means a level 10 mage isn’t just twice as powerful as a level 5, they’re much stronger. At higher levels, even a small increase makes a huge difference. For example, the gap in power between level 100 and 110 is way bigger than the gap between level 10 and 20.

Higher tiers are also rarer, which is reflected in the percentage of people who reach each one—together, all tiers make up about 60% of the population.

If someone is hurt by an A-tier spell, a B-tier healer will have a hard time healing them. An A-tier healer could handle it okay, but an S-tier healer would fix it easily. Healing potions also follow this system, their effectiveness depends on the tier of the person who made them. The higher the tier of the creator, the more serious wounds the potion can heal.
